questions.question: Which compiler macro was introduced in Vue 3.4 to create two-way v-model bindings?
What does `defineExpose` do in a `<script setup>` component?
options: It exports the component for use in other files
It controls which properties and methods are accessible to parent template refs
It declares which props should be passed through to child elements
It enables the component to receive v-model bindings from a parent

questions.question: What is a rule that applies to ALL compiler macros in Vue?
options: They must be imported from 'vue' before use
They can only be used inside the `mounted` lifecycle hook
They must be called at the top level, not inside functions or conditionals
They require TypeScript to be enabled in the project
